sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 26 - Guideline for Election of Operative Dates under Standard Valuation Law and Standard Non-Forfeiture Law.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 26 - Guideline for Election of Operative Dates under Standard Valuation Law and Standard Non-Forfeiture Law

observedSectionWindows.note: Operative-date election rule, dynamic interest-rate language, and the closing background boundary.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 26 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 27 or later guideline files.
Keep the active guideline posture explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the text layer is noisy / encoded.
Treat the guideline as review-only regulatory guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Election of operative dates guideline
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep this slice within page 1 and do not widen into AG 27.

proposedBatches.title: AG 26 election of operative dates slice

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full one-page guideline as one review-only slice because the operative-date rule and the interest-rate boundary should stay together.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full one-page guideline as one review-only slice because the operative-date rule and the interest-rate boundary should stay together.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat the operative-date limitation and required election language as regulatory text when the guideline says it must be followed.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat operative date, dynamic interest rate, and reserve / nonforfeiture references as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat the reserve-rate and nonforfeiture-rate references as structure until the text clearly states a separate duty.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat the interest-rate comparisons and nonforfeiture-rate language as calculation structure until a duty is explicit.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat formula labels and interest-rate boundaries as context until interpretation is explicit.
reviewStandards.prescribedAssumption: Treat stated operative-date limits and mandatory-date language as prescribed when the guideline says they are fixed.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to Standard Valuation Law and Standard Non-Forfeiture Law as cross-reference context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat title, framing, and explanatory notes as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the page 1 window as a review-only slice and do not split it unless a later cleanup pass explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the OCR noise, operative-date election language, and interest-rate boundary language as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 26 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch remains review-only by default.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 26 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag26-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-106 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 26 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch ID.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 26 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 26 planning artifact. Planned batch 106 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
